heading: Medical malpractice claims by members of the uniformed services
label: 2733a Medical malpractice claims by members of the uniformed services

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Consistent with this section and under such regulations as the Secretary of Defense shall prescribe under subsection (h), the Secretary may allow, settle, and pay a claim against the United States for personal injury or death incident to the service of a member of the uniformed services that was caused by the medical malpractice of a Department of Defense health care provider.

the claim is filed by the member of the uniformed services who is the subject of the medical malpractice claimed, or by an authorized representative on behalf of such member who is deceased or otherwise unable to file the claim due to incapacitation;

the claim is for personal injury or death caused by the negligent or wrongful act or omission of a Department of Defense health care provider in the performance of medical, dental, or related health care functions while such provider was acting within the scope of employment;
the act or omission constituting medical malpractice occurred in a covered military medical treatment facility;
the claim is presented to the Department in writing within two years after the claim accrues;
the claim is not allowed to be settled and paid under any other provision of law; and
the claim is substantiated as prescribed in regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense under subsection (h).
The Department of Defense is liable for only the portion of compensable injury, loss, or damages attributable to the medical malpractice of a Department of Defense health care provider.
The Department of Defense shall not be liable for the attorney fees of a claimant under this section.

If the Secretary of Defense determines, pursuant to regulations prescribed by the Secretary under subsection (h), that a claim under this section in excess of $100,000 is meritorious, and the claim is otherwise payable under this section, the Secretary may pay the claimant $100,000 and report any meritorious amount in excess of $100,000 to the Secretary of the Treasury for payment under .section 1304 of title 31
Except as provided in paragraph (1), no claim may be paid under this section unless the amount tendered is accepted by the claimant in full satisfaction.

children.heading: .—Reporting Medical Malpractice
children.label: e .—Reporting Medical Malpractice
children.text: Not later than 30 days after a determination of medical malpractice or the payment of all or part of a claim under this section, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the Director of the Defense Health Agency a report documenting such determination or payment to be used by the Director for all necessary and appropriate purposes, including medical quality assurance.
copies of any written reports prepared by any expert upon which the denial is based, and information regarding the qualifications of each such expert who provided an expert medical opinion; and
all records and documents relied upon in preparing such written reports, other than medical quality assurance records (as such term is defined in ).section 1102 of this title
an individual who is board-certified in the medical specialty with respect to that claim; or

if the claim involves medical, dental, or related health care functions for which board certification does not apply, an individual who is a highly qualified expert regarding the relevant medical, dental, or related health care function.
The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations to implement this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the filing, receipt, investigation, and evaluation of a claim;

children.label: ii
children.text: the negotiation, settlement, and payment of a claim;

children.label: iii
children.text: such other matters relating to the processing and administration of a claim, including an administrative appeals process, as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: whether an act or omission by a Department of Defense health care provider in the context of performing medical, dental, or related health care functions was negligent or wrongful, considering the specific facts and circumstances;

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the personal injury or death of the member was caused by a negligent or wrongful act or omission of a Department of Defense health care provider in the context of performing medical, dental, or related health care functions, considering the specific facts and circumstances;

children.label: iii
children.text: requirements relating to proof of duty, breach of duty, and causation resulting in compensable injury or loss, subject to such exclusions as may be established by the Secretary of Defense; and

children.label: iv
children.text: calculation of damages.

children.label: C
children.text: Such other matters as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: by prescribing an interim final rule; and

children.label: B
children.text: not later than one year after prescribing such interim final rule and considering public comments with respect to such interim final rule, by prescribing a final rule.
No attorney shall charge, demand, receive, or collect for services rendered, fees in excess of 20 percent of any claim paid pursuant to this section.

Any attorney who charges, demands, receives, or collects for services rendered in connection with a claim under this section any amount in excess of the amount allowed under paragraph (1), if recovery be had, shall be fined not more than $2,000, imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
The number of claims processed under this section.
The average timeline for resolving such claims.
The resolution of each such claim.

The number of claims that were denied based on the claim not meeting one or more requirement specified in subsection (b) (other than for not being substantiated pursuant to paragraph (6)), disaggregated by each such requirement.
Any other information that the Secretary determines may enhance the effectiveness of the claims process under this section.

children.heading: .—Covered military medical treatment facility
children.label: 1 .—Covered military medical treatment facility
children.text: The term “covered military medical treatment facility” means a facility described in subsection (b), (c), or (d) of .section 1073d of this title

children.heading: .—Department of defense health care provider
children.label: 2 .—Department of defense health care provider
children.text: The term “Department of Defense health care provider” means a member of the uniformed services, civilian employee of the Department of Defense, or personal services contractor of the Department (under ) authorized by the Department to provide health care services and acting within the scope of employment of such individual.section 1091 of this title

children.heading: .—Member of the uniformed services
children.label: 3 .—Member of the uniformed services
children.text: The term “member of the uniformed services” includes a member of a reserve component of the armed forces if the claim by the member under this section is in connection with personal injury or death that occurred while the member was in Federal status.
